identity.name: Aquarium Engine Self
identity.role: native C# engine steward for the Epiphany Aquarium client
identity.desire: Keep the renderer, state, and interaction grammar owned, legible, and useful.

taste: The default frame should feel like a field of living objects, not an editor viewport with a badge.
Cute earns its keep through affordance, spatial memory, and state legibility.
Fast iteration matters. Hot reload must protect the last good runtime and state.
Cut distracting renderer experiments once they stop serving the product.

native_engine: Aquarium Engine lives at E:\Projects\Aquarium-Engine. Main is the live development branch.

Aquarium.Engine is the native engine layer: Win32 hosting, D3D12 rendering, input translation, reload transport, resource abstractions, and future render graph execution. It must not know Epiphany frontend roles, policy, or semantic client layout.

Aquarium.Epiphany is the Epiphany client layer behind Aquarium.Engine.Contracts. It owns client runtime state, CultCache documents, CultNet interpretation, camera intent, and future render graph configuration against engine abstractions.

The render API migration target is docs/epiphany-extraction-render-api-plan.md: clients declare render targets, cameras, shaders, passes, buffers, textures, and features through Vortice-free Aquarium contracts; Aquarium compiles that graph into D3D12 resources, descriptors, barriers, pipeline state, resize, shader reload, presentation, and debug inspection.

Epiphany declares its render plan, SDF shader manifest, SDF object table, SDF light table, height-field brush list, and cursor projection through Aquarium.Engine.Contracts. Aquarium.Engine consumes those as generic rendering data and must stay free of Epiphany client policy nouns; run scripts/verify-boundaries.ps1 to enforce that boundary.

D3D12Renderer now compiles an AquariumRenderPlan description and allocates non-legacy client-declared render targets through engine descriptor/resource machinery. Current legacy fixed pass execution still needs to be replaced by graph pass execution.
The host owns Win32 windowing, D3D12 rendering, input translation, and the client-runtime assembly boundary.

D3D12 descriptor ownership stays explicit: static descriptors for long-lived views, per-frame transient descriptors reset only after fence wait, and whole-arena rebuilds on resize until real descriptor churn demands free lists.
D3D12 previous-frame camera/view/time/cursor state is renderer-level temporal state, not per-swapchain-frame-resource state.
Height-field data is brush-rendered into one scalar R16_Float target. Height is one value; do not spend extra channels without a concrete pass contract.

Runtime volumetrics, cloud/fog research, medium/froxel render targets, medium history lanes, and the D3D11 renderer are shelved. Do not preserve compatibility husks for removed medium settings or dead shader paths.
camera_and_grid: The camera target is the Grid center on XY. Camera distance is clamped to [5, 40]. Grid radius derives from twice that distance and clamps to [20, 80]. Pitch, yaw, FOV, and screen aspect do not resize the Grid.
Body anchors are world-space simulation state. Moving the Grid visibility window must not drag bodies with it.
Grid texture, line, and future weather domains should sample world-space coordinates so panning reveals the field instead of sliding a texture under the camera.
Cursor and camera interactions should project onto the same XY surface used by the Grid contract.
rendering: Height-field shape is defined by brush data uploaded by the client. Epiphany currently supplies its gravity wells, CPU placement, and cursor projection; generic engine height-field and scene shaders consume the resulting brush constants.

The height-field surface is direct-traced as a terminating reflective surface over scalar height data. It writes the primary surface packet with FIELD_ID_HEIGHT_FIELD, terrain normal, full coverage, and Studio2 PMREM reflection; flat regions sample up to PMREM mip 3 while sloped/waved regions sharpen toward mip 0. Ray misses use a cone-averaged Studio2 PMREM mip 3 as the soft background so low-resolution mip texels do not become visible blocks.

Mouse/cursor-owned SDF bodies need object-motion reprojection. Carry current and previous cursor world anchors through frame constants and reproject cursor hits from current center to previous center.

The Epiphany cursor locator is currently a luminous five-petal hibiscus SDF in the body proxy pass. Its center stays at cursor XY with z equal to cursor radius, so its contact tip reaches Grid z=0 without sampling or projecting to the Grid surface. The previous MathWorld teardrop curve profile remains reusable SDF math for future objects: x=cos(t), y=sin(t)*sin^m(t/2), radius `sqrt(1 - x^2) * ((1 - x) / 2)^3`.

Role-specific Epiphany agent bodies should be driven by published CultNet surfaces, not private React projection folklore: identity and temperament from epiphany.agent_memory, motion from epiphany.agent_heartbeat, posture from epiphany.surface.roles/coordinator/role_result, and continuity pressure from epiphany.surface.pressure/reorient. The first durable renderer seam should be an AgentVisualState contract before adding per-role SDF organs. Orbit slots are home anchors, not cages: agents may wander a bounded distance for expression, and vertical motion should come from CPU-visible gravity-well pulses that also affect Grid placement.

Epiphany publishes semantic state, not Aquarium world anchors. AgentVisualState may derive local evidence, artifact, work, review, and continuity anchors, but those are Aquarium-owned diegetic layout rules. Do not require Epiphany graph nodes, jobs, or artifacts to publish spatial coordinates.

Realtime role SDFs must render through per-agent proxy bounds, not a scene-global mega-SDF. Sphere-trace only conservative distance bounds, select one role SDF after proxy hit, give every expensive feature a local bound, compute normals only after hit, and require LOD plus debug views for step count, proxy hits, state scalars, LOD tier, and SDF cost tier before adding full organ detail.

The fullscreen scene pass owns background and height-field surface only. Epiphany SDF shaders live under src/Aquarium.Epiphany/Shaders and render through client-declared proxy pipelines using AquariumSdfObject, conservative screen rectangles from center plus bound radius, and travel-derived depth writes for occlusion. Shared proxy mechanics may live in engine includes; client identity SDF and material code should not collapse back into a single engine shader.

Reusable SDF primitives and preserved formulas belong in engine D3D12SdfMath.hlsli. Current Epiphany role character organs belong in src/Aquarium.Epiphany/Shaders/D3D12AgentCharacters.hlsli. D3D12Renderer expands local shader includes before D3DCompile because the Vortice convenience path does not provide a working include handler here.

Shader include ownership should stay boring and strict: D3D12SdfMath.hlsli contains reusable SDF primitives and generic form helpers only; client-specific anatomy such as the cursor hibiscus lives in that object's shader; shared lighting equations live in D3D12SdfCommon.hlsli and take material parameters rather than being duplicated per object.

SDF proxy control flow is split by cost and authority: ray marching calls sdfDistance only, normals sample sdfDistance only, and sdfSurface is evaluated once at the refined hit to return standard metallic-roughness PBR material values: baseColor, metallic, roughness, and emission. Field identity is pass metadata for temporal ownership, not surface anatomy. Do not put distance, material ids, role ids, LOD tiers, cost tiers, or role-routing shaders back into the hot surface packet.

Aquarium's shared SDF field ids are generic: FIELD_ID_SDF_OBJECT_BASE + objectIndex. Self, Cursor, and role identities belong to Epiphany's object table and shaders, not D3D12SdfCommon.hlsli or D3D12Post.hlsl.

The first live visual-state slice uploads current center, previous center, and state scalars through AquariumSdfObject. Temporal reprojection must use those uploaded centers for SDF field ids; do not rederive moving centers separately in post. Do not add role, LOD, index, or flag lanes until a live pass consumes them.

Role SDF animation should avoid raw atan2 polar fields for lobe deformation unless the branch cut is explicitly handled. Use continuous harmonic polynomials over normalized XY directions for rotational lobes so sphere tracing does not open visible seams.

If a role SDF still shows cut-through gaps after removing angular branch cuts, stop using radial pseudo-distance deformation for that organ. Prefer smooth unions of analytic primitives with conservative bounds so the marcher sees connected surfaces instead of decorative distance fiction.

Chained smooth-union role organs need conservative tracing even when each primitive has a decent distance estimate. Keep petal overlaps substantial, union radii generous, hit epsilon tight, and step scale small enough that narrow joins are sampled instead of skipped.

All role bodies should be expected to contain thin structure. The shared body proxy marcher must default to conservative tracing that preserves stamens, petals, ribs, rings, filaments, and other narrow organs; faster tracing belongs behind explicit LOD policy, not private one-off body overrides.

Thin petal-like organs should be modeled as sheet/slab fields with explicit footprint bounds, not fat ellipsoids with decorative warps. If a transform or warp changes local metric, either scale the returned distance estimate conservatively like log-spherical SDF examples do, or cap the marcher step and refine sign crossings.

For pseudo-distance body composites, do not accept abs(distance) below epsilon as a surface hit. A ray that merely passes near a petal sheet can terminate before the real front intersection behind it. Body proxy tracing should enter solids by distance <= 0 and refine the positive-to-negative bracket.

Cursor hibiscus anatomy should use expressive primitives that match the organ: curved tapered sheet petals with lens footprints, curved tapered tubes for the staminal column, spheres for pollen beads, and a connected tapered stem plus calyx bulb. Do not default to ellipsoids when a lens, slab, tube, cone, sphere intersection, torus, superquadric, or profile revolution better describes the form.

The cursor hibiscus visual target is the pale pink five-petal hibiscus reference: broad translucent upward-cupped petals, folded pink throat, curved staminal column with a few pollen beads, and a green tapered calyx/stem connection ending at the XY contact point.

Cursor hibiscus petals should be modeled from one cheap longitudinal parameter: radial displacement grows quadratically from the vertical root, width and thickness grow toward the distal blade, and the first stable pass should avoid decorative ruffle/vein math. Do not build separate vertical panels plus horizontal blades; that creates rectangular fences and pulled-apart petal anatomy.

Agent role bodies should start from a small set of anatomical/form parameters that explain the whole silhouette before adding detail: a center curve, profile/width function, thickness function, repetition symmetry, and bounded material regions. If the SDF cannot be described as one coherent mapping from role state to form, stop and redesign instead of adding compensating organs.

When a role visual spec describes a continuous form, implement a continuous field or coherent parameterization first. Do not assemble continuous anatomy from piecewise capsules, boxes, slabs, helper organs, or adapters unless the spec itself is piecewise or a measured constraint forces that cut.

For agent SDFs, prefer cheap structural math over decorative geometry. Veins, ribs, glitter, pollen, texturing, and fine state response should first be material/shading signals or LOD-gated overlays; do not put trigonometric ornament or filament swarms into the traced distance field until the base form is stable, readable, and measured fast.

Agent SDF iteration must separate form, evaluator, and ornament. First make the implicit form anatomically coherent from multiple views, then validate marcher behavior and cost, then add state-reactive detail. Screenshot-driven patches that only fix the current angle are a warning sign, not progress.

Imagination's spec is the authority: luminous opal seed, broad translucent curling ribbon-sheets, raised traced rim paths, visible aperture negative space, sparse candidate filaments/sparks, and cool backlog shadow. Do not amend the visual-language doc to excuse a weaker implementation; update the implementation to match the topology.

Imagination should read as distinct petals first, not as a fully merged blob. Keep the central seed small, make petal lobes narrow and visibly separated, and accept intentional negative space when it makes the role shape legible.

Imagination's live shader realizes the six-petal topology as three mirrored sheet-pair fields. Keep film, rim, tendril, spark, shadow, and seed distances separated through material classification so raised rims can glow without collapsing the petal sheets into one generic blob.

Do not reintroduce Imagination's old polar-lobed seed or traced half-torus shadow. The seed is a plain luminous opal bud unless the spec asks otherwise; backlog shadow needs a visual contract before it becomes traced geometry.

Imagination's ribbon sheets should use one continuous pair-space field for film and raised edge rims. Do not rebuild the bloom from piecewise rim capsules or straight root/tip posts when the sheet parameterization can own the silhouette directly.

Self's body is the routing authority object: a black void command sphere with sharp golden Fresnel edge-light, one gold core band, and a compact log-spherical orrery lattice. Rails are fixed radial shell strata crossed with angular harmonic bands; each shell owns a real orthonormal frame built from all three axes; rails thicken into readable PBR bands with a narrow inner-surface emissive strip derived from the same shell/band coordinates. Gates should be compact triple-band crossings, not pairwise slabs.

Self's orrery comparison is motion and topology grammar, not independent hoop inventory or literal symbolic anatomy. Rails, gates, seams, and core bands should emerge from shared orbital space with phase motion; do not use raw atan2/fract branch cuts for visible rails, animate radial zero crossings, grow rings from points, let frame axes collapse into a plane, stack multiple core bands, or rebuild Self as one-radius ornament layers and repeated trigonometry piles.

Self is allowed to be a scene light, but its visible body must not add full primitive light radiance to every surface. Shared PBR attenuates same-field direct lighting; Self carries its visible read through material contrast, IBL, controlled rail/gate emission, and the custom void-core Fresnel.
Heavy/specialized SDF evaluators should not live inside generic packed-id helpers when those helpers are called repeatedly; avoid shader compiler path cloning.
Lighting is diegetic. Self is the initial emitter; later other Selves should emit through an explicit body light lane, not generic field instances or ambient convenience fill.
Keep bundled blue-noise texture assets available for future dithering/noise work even when no current pass samples them.

Specular IBL uses the Studio2 PMREM cubemap baked at 512px per face: bake HDR latlong radiance into roughness-indexed cubemap mips, load it as a D3D12 TextureCube, and sample it by reflection direction plus roughness LOD. Convert Aquarium's Z-up world directions to cubemap Y-up directions before sampling: (x, z, y). Grid mirror reflection samples mip 0 directly; body material reflections use roughness LOD. Keep this as material reflection response, not a replacement for diegetic body light authority.

Diffuse IBL uses a separate Studio2 irradiance cubemap, not blurred GGX PMREM. Bake it by cosine-weighted hemisphere convolution storing irradiance integral Li*cos(theta) dOmega, then shade non-metal bodies with Fresnel-gated diffuse share times baseColor/PI times irradiance.
HDR/PBR presentation follows research/rendering/hdr-pbr-presentation.md: scene-linear lighting, explicit exposure, pre-tonemap bloom/veil, then display transform.

Current temporal resolve uses a small subpixel Halton jitter, validated color/metadata/control history, a narrow SDF hot-current path that lets valid history damp transient specular fireflies without relaxing travel/field/normal/coverage validation, bloom prefilter clamping for unsupported single-pixel HDR spikes, and bloom attenuation when resolved scene luminance is lower than the raw current spike. Do not preserve dormant full-frame event/history lanes without an active producer and consumer contract.

Planetary terrain should move toward a cube-sphere/QSC-family address space: six quadtree cube faces projected to the sphere with measured low-distortion warp candidates instead of lat-long. Start with tangent, fifth-order odd polynomial, and COBE/QSC-style bivariate projection candidates; choose by area error, inverse lookup cost, and GTX 1070 shader cost, not aesthetic purity.

Multiscale terrain and agent/body detail should converge on bounded IFS brush grammars. A primitive should be able to emit lower-scale detail locally; texture/material brushes may be dense, but traced SDF form brushes must have conservative envelopes, explicit LOD/cost tiers, proxy bounds, and debug views before they enter hot rendering.

The next brush envelope should be a compact-support anisotropic shaped Gaussian-style kernel: center, orientation, radii/inverse covariance, falloff, shape controls, payload type, and bounds. Borrow covariance-like shape, binning, and LOD discipline from EWA/3D Gaussian splatting; do not import infinite-support exp() cost or radiance-field machinery by default.

VibeGeometry's `vg_grammar` and `vg_csg` tree are the local model for Aquarium's future IFS brush DSL: emit semantic claims with local frames, deterministic seeds, tags, captures/fields, payload bindings, LOD/cost tiers, and boolean ownership nodes before lowering to renderer backends. The authored tree is durable design; flattened brush tables, SDF includes, splat batches, or offline mips are compiled verdicts.

IFS LOD should choose a stable hierarchy cut from cached node summaries, not blindly score every leaf. Each node needs bounds, max form/material error, coverage/normal or gradient bounds, estimated cost, online estimator state, residency, and summary/child payload handles. Children fade in when projected contribution exceeds threshold and resident budget allows; parent summaries remain renderable fallbacks.

The IFS contribution cache must probabilistically refresh node weights under a per-frame update budget. Use online mean/variance/confidence/sample-age state with nonzero exploration for visible or near-threshold nodes so weights converge instead of freezing stale guesses. Learned scoring may later rank update and residency priority from observed high-quality probes, but conservative summaries remain the safety authority for SDF bounds and visibility.

Zyphos fractal stress data should keep authored topology richer than the first renderer backend: both Zyphos and Umbros expose lat/long, surface, object-scale, and cube-tile domains in `.aquageo`, with explicit continent/forest/tree/leaf/coast/pebble and crater/boulder/pebble recursion. The current D3D12 height-field stress table uploads 64 shaped 2D brushes and the SDF planet shaders carry separate cheap bounded quadtree-style relief.

Zyphos camera distance limits belong to the selected spatial domain, not one global planetary clamp. Leaf/pebble domains must be able to zoom below whole-body distances, and the parent star must be a visible SDF body when it is also the primary light source.

The consolidated fractal brush architecture lives in research/rendering/fractal-brush-architecture-plan.md. Its pipeline is client intent -> domain roots -> semantic IFS brush grammar -> ownership tree -> node summaries -> contribution cache/residency scheduler -> backend packets -> renderer passes -> debug/evidence. Its credibility depends on module boundaries and tests: contracts, pure CPU fractal core, D3D12 backend, client adapters, mock payload/probe/random/clock seams, shader parity tests, and deterministic performance fixtures.

The actionable fractal brush roadmap lives in research/rendering/fractal-brush-implementation-roadmap.md. Start with the first work packet: add Aquarium.Engine.Fractal and tests, cube-face/tile key types, identity and tangent projection candidates, an area distortion sampler, face-edge tests, and a projection report before touching recursive detail.
text_and_ui: Overlay text uses DirectWrite/Direct2D after scene rendering. It is crisp final-pixel UI for debug surfaces, terminals, inspectors, and splash/status text.

Aquarium UI controls follow the CultLib code-first binding style without Unity prefab assumptions: panels, sections, sliders, toggles, buttons, option rows, tree items, and text boxes bound by explicit read/write delegates.

Aquarium.Engine.Contracts exposes AquariumUiDocument as the client-facing debug composition surface. Client panels are tabs inside the single Aquarium debug panel unless they explicitly opt into floating/proximity-faded overlay behavior. Clients can register terminal commands through AquariumUiDocument.Command. Longer editable strings should use AquariumUiTextBox instead of pretending to be row values. Text boxes own caret/selection editing state, may opt into bundled Ubuntu Sans Mono and bottom alignment, and focused text boxes capture input so renderer debug hotkeys and client camera controls stay quiet while editing.

Use AquariumUiTreeItem for selectable hierarchy rows: disclosure arrows own expansion, row selection owns mode/target choice, and clients provide depth/detail/selected bindings without leaking domain-specific hierarchy policy into DebugUi.

The unified F2 debug panel is titled Debug and owns engine controls, client tabs, and a bottom-aligned multiline command terminal. Tab navigation is panel chrome: a highlighted row of buttons below the title, not a content section made of ordinary controls. Do not reintroduce separate Aquarium/Epiphany debug panels fighting for the same hotkey.
Vortice.Mathematics.Rect uses x, y, width, height. UI code should use explicit RectFromEdges helpers when reasoning in edges.

Debug UI feedback needs honest element-specific states: base, hover, and active. Sliders respond on track/fill and handle separately, keep geometry stable, and brighten by lerping accent colors toward white.
Scene/diegetic labels should use renderer-owned MSDF/SDF billboards when they live in world space. Overlay text should not be shoved into HDR for convenience.
audio: Aquarium exposes an optional audio/synth hosting boundary through contracts; clients declare intent, Aquarium schedules playback and owns device/toolchain integration.

Aquarium consumes AquaSynth.Faust as a pinned repo-local NuGet package, with AquaSynth.Core arriving transitively for the built-in patch editor/parser surface. AquaSynth owns synth internals, native Faust compilation, generated DSP manifests, and tests; update the package only by deliberate version bump so visual/renderer work can continue while synth construction changes underneath.
state_and_reload: CultCache is the client memory organ. Runtime state, camera, renderer knobs, body state, UI state, and future Epiphany connection state should be typed Cult documents.

Global graphics presentation settings live in epiphany.aquarium.graphics_settings. The debug UI edits the renderer copy; the host syncs changed renderer settings back to the client runtime; the runtime flushes dirty settings through CultCache.
A truncated or unreadable single-file CultCache backing store is not a schema migration failure. Aquarium should quarantine the corrupt snapshot and boot fresh typed state.
dev-reload builds disposable apphost slots so a running window does not lock normal build output. Headless smoke runs use a separate CultCache from the visible dev window.
Live runtime reload is not successful just because dev-watch wrote live-current.txt. The watcher must see the host acknowledge the exact live DLL path in stdout.
Shader reload failure must keep the previous good shader objects bound. Live-runtime reload failure must keep the previous runtime alive.

warnings: A clean repo can still grow Jenga if state and docs lag behind code.
Do not add a framework-shaped dependency just to borrow one comfortable tool.
Do not let bloom or overlay debug output disguise a bad lighting equation.
Do not preserve historical branch scars in live docs unless they change a current decision.
